architectureVision.statement: Enable Real Estate Marketplace through an integrated enterprise architecture spanning business, application, data, and technology domains.
drivers: Reduce fragmentation across organizational and technology change.
Make dependencies and ownership explicit across architecture layers.
Improve governance of strategic change and target-state decisions.

goals.name: 2026-Q1 Individual Seller / Landlord outcome goal
goals.description: Improve Estimation-to-listing conversion for Individual Seller / Landlord by advancing strengthen owner acquisition funnels, valuation credibility, and self-serve listing basics. through owner intent capture and listing reliability.

goals.name: 2026-Q1 Brokerage Owner / Agency Growth Manager outcome goal
goals.description: Improve Qualified lead-to-viewing rate for Brokerage Owner / Agency Growth Manager by advancing fix b2b workflow gaps, prove lead quality, and improve listing operations fundamentals. through professional workflow and roi foundation.

goals.name: 2026-Q2 Individual Seller / Landlord outcome goal
goals.description: Prepare to improve Estimation-to-listing conversion for Individual Seller / Landlord by advancing strengthen owner acquisition funnels, valuation credibility, and self-serve listing basics. through owner intent capture and listing reliability.

goals.name: 2026-Q2 Brokerage Owner / Agency Growth Manager outcome goal
goals.description: Prepare to improve Qualified lead-to-viewing rate for Brokerage Owner / Agency Growth Manager by advancing fix b2b workflow gaps, prove lead quality, and improve listing operations fundamentals. through professional workflow and roi foundation.

principles: Business capabilities drive application and technology design.
Information is modeled as a shared enterprise asset.
Technology choices should be standardized unless a domain-specific need justifies variation.
Implementation should be governed against explicit target and transition architectures.

stakeholders.name: Committed Home Buyer / Tenant Seeker
stakeholders.concern: A high-intent consumer accountable for finding a suitable home within budget and time constraints while minimizing regret, hidden costs, and search friction. Economic success is measured by time-to-transaction, budget adherence, and confidence in decision quality. Failure risk is choosing the wrong property, overpaying, missing the right listing, or wasting months in search.

stakeholders.name: Individual Seller / Landlord
stakeholders.concern: A property owner accountable for maximizing sale price or rental yield while minimizing time on market, hassle, vacancy, and intermediary costs. Economic success is measured by net proceeds, occupancy, and speed. Failure risk is underpricing, prolonged time on market, bad tenant or buyer quality, and low-quality lead flow.

stakeholders.name: Brokerage Owner / Agency Growth Manager
stakeholders.concern: A professional real estate operator accountable for revenue growth, agent productivity, listing acquisition, and ROI on marketing spend. Economic success is driven by closings, commission volume, listing share, and cost per qualified lead. Failure risk is weak lead quality, poor response performance, low listing visibility, and churn from the platform due to poor unit economics.

stakeholders.name: Broker Software / Integration Partner
stakeholders.concern: A B2B integration partner whose software is used by brokerages and agents to create and manage listings before those listings are imported into the marketplace. They are accountable for reliable data exchange, workflow fit with brokers, and maintaining their strategic position in the brokerage stack. Economic success is driven by retained broker accounts, listing volume flowing through integrations, and expansion into value-added data or workflow services. Failure risk is broken imports, schema mismatches, stale listing sync, duplicate inventory, or brokers bypassing them and publishing directly to the platform.

stakeholders.name: Mortgage Lender / Data Monetization Partner
stakeholders.concern: A partner accountable for acquiring high-intent housing customers or monetizing property and market intelligence through the platform. Economic success is driven by funded loans, qualified applications, data product sales, or partner ROI. Failure risk is poor intent matching, compliance issues, low conversion from referred demand, or weak data trustworthiness.
